ollama_format.title: StructuredSmokeText

structured_output_schema.description: Structured smoke output schema for one text response.
structured_output_schema.title: StructuredSmokeText

parsed_response.response_text: The data reveals systematic patterns in household adoption of social learning (household-SL = true) across simulation timesteps. From timesteps 0 to 6, no households engaged in social learning, indicating an initial phase dominated by individual decision-making without observational adaptation. At timestep 7, the first instances of social learning emerge, with counts beginning at 2 households and fluctuating moderately through timesteps 8–11 (values ranging from 0 to 4). A notable increase occurs at timestep 12 (11 households), establishing a rising trend that culminates in a stabilization phase by timesteps 28–29. The maximum count observed is 54 households (final row), following a peak of 52 at timestep 21 and gradual decline in later years. This trajectory suggests accelerating knowledge propagation in mid-simulation years, with diminishing returns in later stages as broader adoption saturates available agents. Variations in intermediate counts (e.g., 41–42 households at timesteps 23–25) reflect stochastic interactions typical of spatially constrained learning dynamics. The model's framework—highlighting heterogeneous behavioral strategies and social learning radius constraints—aligns with these trends, as agents self-categorized under E-RO-SL1 (n = 20) adapt through observational feedback in clustered settings.
